description: Rings or pieces of squid lightly coated and fried until crisp. It is commonly served as an appetizer with lemon and dipping sauce.
preparation_steps: Clean and slice the squid into rings or pieces.
Coat lightly with seasoned flour or another dry coating.
Fry quickly in hot oil until just crisp and lightly golden.
Drain, season, and serve immediately with lemon and sauce.

health_advice.benefits: Provides seafood protein and is often served in small appetizer portions.
health_advice.risks: Deep-frying raises fat and calorie content, and overcooked squid can become tough. Sodium may also be high.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Keep portions moderate and pair with lemon or a lighter sauce instead of heavy creamy dips.

dietary_notes.note: This is a seafood dish. Gluten-free versions are possible if the coating and fryer are gluten-free.

allergens: Egg or milk in some batters
